metadata.seed: Why live without her? Sure, I can live without her. But I just don't want to. She is the only one I'll ever truly love. What's the point if I'll never love another the same way? Oneitis? Maybe. But I've met girls after her. None interest me enough to even bother. She is just so special to me...

patient.query: Why live without her? Sure, I can live without her. But I just don't want to. She is the only one I'll ever truly love. What's the point if I'll never love another the same way? Oneitis? Maybe. But I've met girls after her. None interest me enough to even bother. She is just so special to me...

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re carrying a heavy, almost all‑encompassing grief, and that’s completely understandable—when someone feels like the only person who truly understands you, the idea of moving on can feel impossible. Try to give yourself permission to feel that loss without judging it as a failure; grief is a process, not a verdict. In the meantime, focus on small, self‑loving habits—exercise, creative outlets, or reconnecting with friends—to rebuild a sense of identity outside of that one relationship. Remember, love can take many forms, and while the depth you felt may be unique, it doesn’t mean you can’t find meaningful connections that honor your worth and bring you joy.
